seoTitle: How to Set Up a Jira Account and Create a Project
metaDescription: A beginner's walkthrough for signing up for Jira and creating your first project, so you can start tracking issues right away.
intro: Getting started with Jira means setting up your account and creating your first project, both of which are quick if you know the right steps. This guide walks through account creation and initial project setup for a new user.

steps.heading: Sign up for a Jira account
steps.body: Go to Jira's sign-up page and create an account using your email address or an existing Atlassian login.

steps.heading: Choose your site name
steps.body: Set up your Jira site with a unique name, which becomes part of your team's dedicated URL.

steps.heading: Select a project template
steps.body: Choose a project template that fits your work style, such as Scrum, Kanban, or a business project template.

steps.heading: Name and configure your project
steps.body: Give your project a name and key, then adjust any initial settings before it's created.

steps.heading: Invite your team
steps.body: Add teammates to the project so they can start collaborating on issues right away.

faq.question: Is Jira free to start with?
faq.answer: Jira offers a free tier for small teams, with paid plans available as your team or feature needs grow.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Scrum and Kanban project template?
faq.answer: Scrum templates are built around sprints and a backlog, while Kanban templates focus on continuous flow without fixed sprint cycles.

faq.question: Can I change my project template after creating it?
faq.answer: Some settings can be adjusted later, but switching the core project type can be more involved, so it's worth choosing carefully at setup.

faq.question: Do I need to invite my whole team right away?
faq.answer: No, you can create the project first and invite teammates whenever you're ready to start collaborating.
